transcript.text: add mobile support to @index.html . ensure footer doesnt overlap with chart, main title doesnt go past right edge of the screen

Two issues:

1. **Footer overlap**: `html, body { overflow: hidden; height: 100% }` + `body { justify-content: center }` â†’ on mobile content taller than viewport gets clipped/centered, footer pushed off or overlapping.
2. **Title overflow**: `h1` at `4.6rem` ("catbotbench" 10 chars) overflows narrow screens.

Fix: mobile media query (allow scroll, drop center-justify) + clamp h1 size.

transcript.name: edit
diff: +    html, body { overflow: auto; height: auto; min-height: 100%; }

transcript.text:
Done. Changes:

- **h1**: `clamp(2.5rem, 10vw, 4.6rem)` + `overflow-wrap: break-word` â†’ scales down on narrow screens, never overflows right edge.
- **Mobile `@media (max-width: 640px)`**:
  - `html/body`: `overflow: auto; height: auto; min-height: 100%` + `body justify-content: flex-start` â†’ page scrolls instead of clipping, footer no longer overlaps chart.
  - Smaller hero padding, lead font, chart height (320â†’220), column min-width (56â†’44), model name + score font.
  - `#agi` wraps, `#dvdcat` shrinks (320â†’180), tooltip max-width capped to viewport.

No JS changes â€” chart already `overflow-x: auto` for many columns.
agi detector box overlaps with the <hr>, model names and providers logos dont move when scrolling the chart horizontally :c

Two problems, two fixes:

1. **Labels don't scroll with chart**: `.chart` and `.labels` are separate siblings â€” chart scrolls, labels stay. Wrap both in one scroll container so they move together.
2. **AGI box touches footer hr**: not enough bottom gap onmobile.

transcript.name: edit
diff: +    #agi { flex-wrap: wrap; gap: 8px; padding: 8px 12px; font-size: 0.84rem; margin-top: 20px; margin-bottom: 20px; }

transcript.text:
Done. Changes:

- **Synced scroll**: wrapped `.chart` + `.labels` in `.chart-scroll` (one `overflow-x: auto` container). Both now scroll together â€” logos and names track bars. Removed `overflow-x` from `.chart`, added `min-width: 100%` to both rows so they grow identically (same gap, same `min-width` cols â†’ equal width â†’ aligned).
- **AGI/footer gap**: moved `margin-top: 30px` from inline to CSS, added `margin-bottom: 20px` + `margin-top: 20px` on mobile so the box clears the footer `border-top` (hr).

Works on desktop too â€” if many models overflow, labels now scroll with chart instead of getting clipped by `overflow: hidden`.

lastly, when scrolling on mobile it seems that you can accelerate and briefly scroll out of the main page bounds into a white background before it forces the 'camera' back. could you explain why this happens and ensure this won't happen to users on mobile?

and also the bouncing dvd cat seems to be confined into a thinner box than necessary - top and left side are a little further from the wall, and bottom and right side are MUCH further than the actual wall. look into that plz
Two bugs. Let me inspect the catimage first.

Confirmed the cat image bug. Image is 128Ă—128, content bbox `(7,9,121,119)` â€” nearly centered, small padding. So the "MUCH further on bottom/right" is **not** from the image.

Root cause: **JS `SIZE=320` constant doesn't match mobile CSS** (`#dvdcat { 180Ă—180 }`). On a 390px phone, `x_max = 390-320 = 70`, but element is 180 wide â†’ right edge maxes at 250, leaving ~140px dead zone on right + bottom. Desktop is 320/320 so only the small image padding shows (the "a little further").

## Overscroll (white bounce) explanation

iOS Safari **rubber-band scrolling**: when a scroll container reaches its boundary and you keep flinging, the browser paints the overscroll area using the `<html>` element's background. Your gradient lives on `<body>`, and `<html>` had no background â†’ defaults to **white**. So the bounce reveals white before snapping back. Two fixes layered: (1) `overscroll-behavior: none` on `html` disables the bounce at the root scroller (iOS 16+), and (2) give `html` a dark background so even if something flashes, it's not white.

## Cat fix

Crop the image to its actual alpha content via canvas (removes the small padding too), size the element to the cropped content, and animate bounds from the **real rendered size** â€” so it works at any size, desktop or mobile.

transcript.name: edit
diff: -  html, body { margin: 0; padding: 0; height: 100%; overflow: hidden; }
+  html, body { margin: 0; padding: 0; height: 100%; overflow: hidden; }
-  #dvdcat img { width: 100%; height: 100%; object-fit: contain; display: block; }
+  #dvdcat img { width: 100%; height: 100%; object-fit: contain; display: block; }

transcript.name: edit
diff: +      let x = Math.random() * Math.max(0, window.innerWidth - dispW);
+      let y = Math.random() * Math.max(0, window.innerHeight - dispH);
+ // fallback: animate the existing using its rendered size
+    // crop image to its visible (non-transparent) content so the sprite
+    // truly touches the walls, then animate using the real rendered size.
+      try { data = ctx.getImageData(0, 0, cw, ch).data; } catch (e) { fallback(); return; }
+      let minX = cw, minY = ch, maxX = 0, maxY = 0, found = false;
+      cropped.getContext('2d').drawImage(probe, minX, minY, contentW, contentH, 0, 0, contentW, contentH);

transcript.text: Done. **Overscroll / white bounce**: - `html { overscroll-behavior: none; background-color: #0a0908; }` â€” kills root rubber-band (iOS 16+), and any residual flash is dark not white. **DVD cat**: - Was: `SIZE=320` hardcode + `object-fit: contain` letterboxing + asymmetric because mobile rendered 180px but bounds used 320 â†’ ~140px dead zone on right/bottom. - Now: loads image into offscreen canvas, scans alpha to find real content bbox `(7,9,121,119)`, crops to 114Ă—110, swaps the `` for that cropped canvas, sizes the element to the cropped content (scaled to TARGET: 160 mobile / 260 desktop), and animates bounds from `innerWidth/Height - dispW/dispH`. Cat now touches all four walls exactly. Fallback: if canvas/taint fails, animates the original img using its rendered size (still no dead zone).
also decrease model name text size on mobile significantly
